name: hakrawler
title: hakrawler

commands.name: hakrawler

arguments.description: Show the source of URL based on where it was found. E.g. href, form, script, etc.
arguments.raw: -s	Show the source of URL based on where it was found. E.g. href, form, script, etc.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# hakrawler --help
 Usage of hakrawler:
 -d int
   	Depth to crawl. (default 2)
 -h string
   	Custom headers separated by two semi-colons. E.g. -h "Cookie: foo=bar;;Referer: http://example.com/" 
 -insecure
   	Disable TLS verification.
 -json
   	Output as JSON.
 -proxy string
   	Proxy URL. E.g. -proxy http://127.0.0.1:8080
 -s	Show the source of URL based on where it was found. E.g. href, form, script, etc.
 -size int
   	Page size limit, in KB. (default -1)
 -subs
   	Include subdomains for crawling.
 -t int
   	Number of threads to utilise. (default 8)
 -timeout int
   	Maximum time to crawl each URL from stdin, in seconds. (default -1)
 -u	Show only unique urls.

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=57fba9f2fd0ec0ac44399fd7ab003f27f14b271e, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Web crawler designed for easy, quick discovery of endpoints and assets
Fast golang web crawler for gathering URLs and JavaSript file locations.
This is basically a simple implementation of the awesome Gocolly
library.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.

Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
